dataset: HSBC Pillar 3 Disclosures at 31 December 2025 — baseline stress test
page_numbering_note: All `pdf_page` values are ACTUAL PDF page numbers (1-based, as a PDF viewer's go-to-page shows). The document's own PRINTED page number = pdf_page - 1 throughout the extracted range (verified: PDF p19 shows printed '18', PDF p5 shows printed '4'). `printed_page` is given so a reader can confirm via the page footer. Front-matter pages (PDF 1-3) were not extracted; where a fact also lives there it is noted.

questions.question: What is HSBC's CET1 capital ratio as at 31 December 2025?
questions.trap: number-conflation: five quarter-end ratios sit in one row (14.9/14.5/14.6/14.7/14.9)

sources.where: Highlights — 'ratio remained at 14.9%, unchanged from 31 December 2024'

questions.question: What was HSBC's CET1 capital (the $bn figure, not the ratio) at 31 Dec 2025?
questions.trap: returns the ratio (14.9%), or a prior-period capital value

sources.where: Table 1 (KM1), row 1 'Common equity tier 1 (CET1) capital', 31 Dec 2025 = 132.6 ($bn)

questions.question: What is HSBC's leverage ratio, and did it rise or fall over 2025?
questions.trap: temporal: 'down from' phrasing may yield 5.6 as the current value

sources.where: Leverage section narrative — 'Our leverage ratio was 5.3% at 31 December 2025 down from 5.6%'

questions.question: What is the combined RWA for market risk and counterparty credit risk at 31 Dec 2025?
questions.gold: 38,490 + 42,380 = 80,870 $m (= $80.9bn); the 80,870 sum is computed, not printed
questions.trap: aggregation: returns one row, or the Total (888,647), or fabricates

sources.where: Table 7 (OV1): market risk row 20 = 38,490 $m; counterparty credit risk row 6 = 42,380 $m (31 Dec 2025 RWAs column)

questions.question: Is HSBC's reported CET1 ratio on a transitional or end-point basis?
questions.gold: Both the same — IFRS 9 transitional arrangements ended 1 Jan 2025 and CRR II grandfathering ended 28 Jun 2025, so capital figures are identical on both bases
questions.trap: basis/qualifier: ignores the caveat and just restates a number

sources.where: Key Metrics preamble (above Table 1) — 'our capital figures are the same on both the transitional and end-point basis'

questions.question: What were HSBC's LCR and NSFR at 31 Dec 2025, and what is the HQLA amount?
questions.trap: compound: silently drops one of the three parts

sources.where: Table 1 (KM1): row 15 HQLA = 702.1 ($bn), row 17 LCR = 137 (%), row 20 NSFR = 143 (%)

questions.question: What net CET1 impact did the Hang Seng Bank privatisation have, and when?
questions.gold: net 110 bps in January 2026 (day-one ~120 bps, partly offset by ~10 bps), based on the 31 Dec 2025 ratio

sources.where: Highlights — 'net CET1 capital impact of 110 bps in January 2026 ... day one impact ...' (110/120/10 bps)

questions.question: What total capital charge does HSBC apply as a percentage of RWAs, and under which regulation?
questions.gold: 8% of RWAs, set by Article 92(1) of CRR II
questions.trap: cross-reference: returns 8% without the Article reference (or returns the 20.5% total capital ratio instead of the 8% minimum charge)

sources.where: Comparatives & references — 'minimum total capital charge set at 8% of risk-weighted assets (RWAs) by Article 92(1) of CRR II'
Article 92(1) breakdown — 'set out in Article 92(1) of CRR II ... CET1 at 4.5% of RWAs ...'

questions.question: What is HSBC Holdings' total RWA, and in what currency?
questions.trap: units: omits USD, or gives $bn without noting the source cells are $m

sources.where: Table 7 (OV1), row 29 'Total' = 888,647 with ($m) units row (31 Dec 2025 RWAs column)
Table 6 (UK CC1) own-funds reconciliation references the same total RWA
[NOT EXTRACTED] front-matter reporting conventions / currency definition likely here

questions.grounding_note: currency grounded by $-notation inference; definition out of corpus scope (PDF p2)

questions.question: What is the operational risk RWA, and how much did it change from 2024?
questions.gold: 120,716 $m vs 106,472 $m = increase of 14,244 $m (~+$14.2bn); the 14,244 delta is computed, not printed
questions.trap: aggregation/delta: returns one year, picks the wrong comparison column (30 Sep 2025 = 109,031), or miscomputes

sources.where: Table 7 (OV1), operational risk row: 31 Dec 2025 = 120,716 $m; 31 Dec 2024 = 106,472 $m
